_about: Golden TUI screen-signature vectors for the reproit-tui Go SDK parity gate. TUI signatures live in a SEPARATE namespace from the a11y golden vectors in <repo>/signature_vectors.json: the descriptor source is the rendered terminal screen (the VT cell grid), not a Node role tree (see docs/signature.md, the 'Terminal and instrumented surfaces' sub-contract, around lines 332-341). These vectors therefore bind the SDK to crates/reproit/src/backends/tui.rs, NOT to signature_vectors.json.
_derivation: Every expected_sig / expected_fp below was produced by the REAL tui.rs code, not by a hand-port. A temporary, #[ignore]'d test `gen_tui_golden` was added to the `tests` module in crates/reproit/src/backends/tui.rs, calling the crate's own structural_sig(contents, cursor) and content_fingerprint(contents, cursor) on each `contents` string below, and printing the results. It was run with `cargo test -p reproit gen_tui_golden -- --nocapture --ignored`, the output transcribed here, and the temporary test then removed (tui.rs is unchanged from HEAD). To regenerate, re-add that test and re-run. The `contents` field is the exact vt100 screen().contents() string for the screen; the SDK reproduces that text model in ScreenContents.Text(), then hashes it with the same FNV-1a primitive.

_fields.name: human label for the case
_fields.contents: the vt100 screen().contents() string (rows joined by \n, per-row + trailing-blank-row whitespace trimmed)
_fields.expected_sig: tui.rs structural_sig(contents, cursor): the canonical TUI state signature
_fields.expected_fp: tui.rs content_fingerprint(contents, cursor): the ephemeral Layer-1 effect token

_relationships: Cross-vector facts the spec promises, asserted by the parity test: login_en == login_de (locale-invariant layout); count1 == count3 == count7 (all POS1 collapse) but count0 (ZERO), count1 (POS1), count12 (POS2) are three distinct sigs; hits100 == hits101 sig (same POS3 bucket + same skeleton) while their fingerprints DIFFER (value-only effect); login_en sig != login_en_cur3 sig (cursor cell is structural).

vectors.name: plain_screen
